headline: Exclusive-ASML plots future of chipmaking tools for AI beyond EUV
summary: ASML Holding has ambitious plans to expand its line of chipmaking equipment into several new products to capture more of the rapidly growing market for artificial intelligence chips, a senior executive told Reuters.  More than a decade in development, ASML is the only maker of extreme ‌ultraviolet, or EUV, equipment, which is crucial for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co and Intel in manufacturing the world's most advanced AI chips.  ASML has poured billions of dollars into ‌developing the EUV systems, has a next-generation product nearing production and is researching a third potential generation.

content.title: Exclusive-ASML plots future of chipmaking tools for AI beyond EUV
content.summary: ASML Holding has ambitious plans to expand its line of chipmaking equipment into several new products to capture more of the rapidly growing market for artificial intelligence chips, a senior executive told Reuters.  More than a decade in development, ASML is the only maker of extreme ‌ultraviolet, or EUV, equipment, which is crucial for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co and Intel in manufacturing the world's most advanced AI chips.  ASML has poured billions of dollars into ‌developing the EUV systems, has a next-generation product nearing production and is researching a third potential generation.

headline: $81.04 Bn Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) Markets - Global Forecast to 2030 with IBM, Cisco, AWS, Google, Microsoft, Oracle, HPE, Intel, Hitachi, and SAP Leading
summary: The global AIoT market is projected to grow from USD 25.44 billion in 2025 to USD 81.04 billion by 2030, driven by the integration of AI and IoT, enhancing real-time analytics. Key growth factors include 5G rollout, edge computing advancements, and low-cost sensors. Smart city initiatives and Industry 4.0 are accelerating demand, though challenges like data security and implementation costs persist. Asia-Pacific is expected to lead in growth. Major players include IBM, Cisco, Google, and Microso
